domain: Trinidadian steel pan tuning (single oil-drum head hammered into note sections; tuner forces section's fundamental + higher modes into integer-ratio harmonic alignment, not natural)
specific_mechanism: Single shared elastic substrate carrying N notes; per-note, the fundamental and several overtones are constrained to integer-ratio harmonic series via tuner-applied curvature/stiffness corrections.
llm_application: PTCH (Pan-Tuned Concept Heads): a multi-head attention fine-tuning rule that constrains each head's top-K singular directions (a fundamental + secondary 'overtones') to integer-ratio multiples in the embedding space, regularized by a harmonic-alignment loss; inter-head 'grooves' enforce orthogonality. Each head becomes a single 'note' with explicit harmonic structure.
novelty_claim: Multi-task disentangling (2503.05320), MoE routing, SVD-LoRA all separate or rank-reduce; none impose a harmonic-series integer-ratio constraint between the fundamental and overtone singular directions within a head. PTCH is uniquely a within-head music-theoretic harmonic-lock constraint.
